packaging_version_control.json.title: Python Packaging & Version Control Master Guide
packaging_version_control.json.description: Complete lessons on Python packaging (pip, virtualenv) and Git/GitHub version control with executable examples

lessons.title: Virtual Environments with virtualenv & pip
lessons.commands:
pip install virtualenv
virtualenv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # Linux/Mac
venv\Scripts\activate   # Windows
pip install requests numpy
pip freeze > requirements.txt
pip install -r requirements.txt

example.setup_env.sh:
#!/bin/bash

echo "🗂️  Creating virtual environment..."
virtualenv venv
source venv/bin/activate

echo "📦 Installing core dependencies..."
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install requests==2.31.0 numpy==1.24.3 pandas==2.0.3

echo "💾 Saving requirements..."
pip freeze > requirements.txt

echo "✅ Environment ready! (venv activated)"
pip list

lessons.title: Git Essentials & Project Initialization
lessons.objective: Version control setup and basic workflow
lessons.commands:
git init
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "email@example.com"
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git status
git log --oneline

example.git_init.sh:
#!/bin/bash

git init
git config user.name "Developer"
git config user.email "dev@example.com"

echo "# My Python Project" > README.md
echo "version = '0.1.0'" > __init__.py

git add .
git commit -m "🎉 Initial project setup with README and __init__.py"
git log --oneline

lessons.title: Python Packaging with pyproject.toml

example.pyproject.toml:
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=61.0.0", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "data-processor"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Process and analyze data files"
readme = "README.md"
license = {text = "MIT"}
requires-python = ">=3.8"
classifiers = [
  "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
  "License :: OSI Approved :: MIT License",
  "Operating System :: OS Independent",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/username/data-processor"
"Bug Tracker" = "https://github.com/username/data-processor/issues"

[project.dependencies]
requests = ">=2.28.0"
numpy = ">=1.21.0"
pandas = ">=1.5.0"

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = [
  "pytest>=7.0",
  "black",
  "flake8",
  "mypy",
]

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
where = ["src"]
include = ["data_processor*"]
example.src/data_processor/__init__.py:
"""[1mData Processor[0m - Process and analyze data files

[3mversion 0.1.0[0m
"""

__version__ = "0.1.0"

from .core import process_data
from .utils import load_csv

lessons.commands:
mkdir -p src/data_processor
touch src/data_processor/__init__.py
pip install build
python -m build
pip install -e .

lessons.title: GitHub Integration & CI/CD
lessons.commands: git remote add origin https://github.com/username/repo.git\ngit branch -M main\ngit push -u origin main

example..github/workflows/ci.yml:
name: Python CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [ main ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ main ]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: [3.8, 3.9, 3.10, 3.11]

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      uses: actions/setup-python@v4
      with:
        python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt
        pip install -e .[dev]
    - name: Run tests
      run: |
        pytest tests/ -v
example.Makefile:
.PHONY: install test build publish clean

install:
	pip install -e .[dev]

test:
	pytest tests/ -v

build:
	python -m build

publish:
	twine upload dist/*

clean:
	rm -rf dist/ build/ .eggs/ *.egg-info/

example..gitignore:
# Byte-compiled / optimized / DLL files
__pycache__/
*.py[cod]
*$py.class

# C extensions
*.so

# Distribution / packaging
.Python
build/
develop-eggs/
dist/
.sdist/
.wheels/
*.egg-info/
.installed.cfg
*.eggs/

# PyInstaller
#  Usually these files are written by a python script from a template
#  before PyInstaller builds the exe, so as to inject date/other infos into it.
*.manifest
*.spec

# Virtual environments
venv/
env/
ENV/
env.bak/
venv.bak/

# IDE
.vscode/
.idea/
*.swp
*.swo

# OS generated files
.DS_Store
.DS_Store?
._*
.Spotlight-V100
.Trashes
ehthumbs.db
Thumbs.db

# Pip
pip-log.txt
pip-delete-this-directory.txt

# Environment variables
.env
.venv
env/

# Testing
.pytest_cache/
.coverage
htmlcov/
.tox/
.nox/
.coverage.*
.cache
nosetests.xml
coverage.xml

# Translations
*.mo
*.pot

lessons.title: Build, Test, Publish Workflow
lessons.commands:
pip install build twine
python -m build
pip install -e .[dev]
pytest tests/
twine check dist/*
twine upload --repository testpypi dist/*

example.publish.sh:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Build
pip install build twine
python -m build

# Test install
pip install dist/*.whl

# Verify
twine check dist/*

# Test upload (uncomment for real publish)
# twine upload --repository testpypi dist/*

echo "✅ Build successful! Ready for PyPI upload."

# Real publish (uncomment when ready)
# twine upload dist/*
example.tests/test_core.py:
import pytest
from data_processor.core import process_data


def test_process_data():
    """Test core data processing function."""
    result = process_data([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])
    assert len(result) == 5
    assert result[0] == 1
    assert sum(result) == 15


def test_invalid_input():
    with pytest.raises(ValueError):
        process_data([])

project_structure.template:
myproject/
├── src/
│   └── myproject/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── core/
│       └── utils/
├── tests/
├── docs/
├── .github/workflows/
├── pyproject.toml
├── README.md
├── requirements.txt
├── .gitignore
└── LICENSE
project_structure.init_command: mkdir -p src/myproject/{core,utils} tests docs ".github/workflows" && touch pyproject.toml requirements.txt .gitignore README.md LICENSE && git init

quick_start.one_liner: git clone <repo> && cd <repo> && python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate && pip install -r requirements.txt && pip install -e .
quick_start.full_setup: git init && virtualenv venv && source venv/bin/activate && pip install -r requirements.txt && git add . && git commit -m 'Initial setup'
